response: sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. she decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. if a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? remember to apply the discount to each item separately. *** reach the customer's total payment by calculating the discounted prices of each item and then summing them. the handcrafted table is discounted by 15%, so its discounted price is $200 - (15% of $200) = $170. the factory-made chairs are also discounted by 15%, so their discounted price is $150 - (15% of $150) = $127.50. the total amount the customer pays is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. *** the promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. the handcrafted table is originally priced at $200 and the factory-made chairs are originally priced at $150. the discount is applied to each item separately, so the table is discounted by 15% and the chairs by 15%. the discounted price of the table is $200 - (15% of $200) = $170. the discounted price of the chairs is $150 - (15% of $150) = $127.50. the total amount the customer pays is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. *** the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. *** the customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week.
